revision_summary:
Four targeted fixes required:

1. SCHEMA MISUSE — elderly_low_tech_protocol misapplied twice (cherry_pick_moments[3] and multi_touch_patterns[0]). Yasser shows no elderly/low-tech signals; he navigated a desktop website and sent screenshots successfully. Replace with the general bill_collection 2-minute pivot rule. Do not invoke elderly_low_tech_protocol unless the trigger condition (customer cannot navigate smartphone within 1-2 attempts) is met.

2. MISSED VERIFY GAP — title_co_owners was never collected. Parker moved from roof type to bill collection to scheduling without asking if anyone else is on the title. This is a hard required verify item per the schema and a direct no-show risk. Add as a cherry_pick_moments entry (verify_gap classification) and include in handoff_brief.unresolved_concerns.

3. LEAD-TYPE AMBIGUITY — multi_touch_patterns[0].stewart_read and coaching_focus[1].why both assert the bill hunt was 'pure time burn' and invoke paid-lead paycheck consequences without knowing the lead type. The schema explicitly flags lead_type as unknown metadata. Revise to: 'if this is a paid lead, the bill was required work — the coaching gap is efficiency, not the effort itself; if organic, the bill hunt was deferrable.' Make the paid-lead paycheck framing conditional throughout.

4. ROOFING CROSS-SELL CHARACTERIZATION — rep_intent.evidence[1] and cherry_pick_moments[1] label Parker's roofing response as 'reactive.' The transcript shows Parker proactively identified Ion's roofing capability, mentioned bundling savings, and offered to include a roof quote — this is an incomplete proactive move, not a reactive one. Revise to: 'partially executed cross-sell — Parker identified the signal and mentioned roofing capability but did not explicitly co-route to the roofing team as the schema requires.' This distinction matters for the coaching conversation (training gap vs awareness gap).
